subject: Physics
title: System of Particles and Rotational Motion

questions.text: The point at which the entire mass of a system may be considered to be concentrated is:
questions.explanation: Center of mass is the point representing the translational motion of the system.

questions.text: Torque is the rotational analogue of:
questions.explanation: Torque produces angular acceleration just as force produces linear acceleration.

questions.text: The moment of inertia of a body depends on:
options: Distribution of mass about the axis
questions.explanation: I = Σ m_i r_i².

questions.text: Angular momentum (L) is defined as:
questions.explanation: Cross product of position vector and linear momentum.

questions.text: The rotational kinetic energy of a body is:
questions.explanation: Analogue to ½mv².

questions.text: A couple produces:
questions.explanation: A couple consists of two equal and opposite forces, so net force is zero but net torque is non-zero.

questions.text: The radius of gyration (k) is related to moment of inertia (I) as:
questions.explanation: k = sqrt(I/m).

questions.text: The Theorem of Parallel Axes states:
questions.explanation: Relates MOI about any axis to MOI about a parallel axis through CM.

questions.text: If net external torque is zero, which quantity is conserved?
questions.explanation: dL/dt = τ_ext. If τ_ext = 0, L is constant.

questions.text: Moment of inertia of a ring of mass M and radius R about its diameter is:
questions.explanation: About the axis perpendicular to plane is MR²; by perpendicular axis theorem, it's half about the diameter.

questions.text: The cross product A x B is:
questions.explanation: Result is a vector perpendicular to both A and B.

questions.text: Center of mass of a uniform rod lies at:
questions.explanation: For symmetric bodies, CM coincides with the geometric center.

questions.text: Angular acceleration (α) is equal to:
questions.explanation: Rate of change of angular velocity.

questions.text: In pure rolling motion of a sphere, the velocity of the point of contact is:
questions.explanation: Point of contact is at instantaneous rest (v - ωr = 0).

questions.text: The unit of torque is:
questions.explanation: Newton-meter (dimensionally same as Work).

questions.text: An ice skater spinning on one leg pulls her arms in to:
questions.explanation: Decreasing MOI increases angular velocity to conserve angular momentum.

questions.text: Which theorem is valid only for planar bodies (lamina)?
questions.explanation: I_z = I_x + I_y applies only to 2D objects.

questions.text: Work done by torque is:
questions.explanation: Analogue to F·d.

questions.text: MOI of a solid sphere about its diameter is:
questions.explanation: Standard derived value for a solid sphere.

questions.text: Relationship between Torque (τ) and MOI (I) is:
questions.explanation: Rotational analogue of F = ma.

questions.text: For a system of particles, internal forces:
questions.explanation: Internal forces occur in action-reaction pairs and cancel out in the net force sum.

questions.text: Angular momentum is a _____ quantity.
questions.explanation: Direction is along the axis of rotation.

questions.text: Unit of moment of inertia is:
questions.explanation: Mass times distance squared.

questions.text: A disc and a ring of same mass and radius roll down an incline. Which reaches first?
questions.explanation: Disc has lower MOI (½MR²) than Ring (MR²), so it has higher acceleration.

questions.text: The position of Center of Mass of a system does not depend on:
questions.explanation: Internal forces do not change the CM position.

questions.text: The vector product î x î is:
questions.explanation: Cross product of a vector with itself is zero.

questions.text: Power in rotational motion is:
questions.explanation: Rotational analogue of F·v.

questions.text: If a rigid body is rotating about a fixed axis, every particle of the body has the same:
questions.explanation: All particles sweep the same angle in the same time.

questions.text: Moment of inertia of a solid cylinder of radius R about its own axis is:
questions.explanation: Same as a disc of the same mass and radius.

questions.text: The cross product ĵ x î is:
questions.explanation: Order is reversed, so sign changes.

questions.text: Motion of center of mass depends only on:
questions.explanation: Σ F_ext = M a_cm.

questions.text: The ratio of rotational KE to total KE for a rolling solid sphere is:
questions.explanation: K_rot = ½(2/5 MR²)ω². K_trans = ½Mv². Ratio = (2/5) / (1 + 2/5) = 2/7.

questions.text: Torque is also known as:
questions.explanation: It measures the turning effect of a force.

questions.text: Which of the following has the highest MOI if mass and radius are same?
questions.explanation: Ring (MR²) > Cylinder/Disc (0.5MR²) > Sphere (0.4MR²).

questions.text: Angular momentum is conserved when:
questions.explanation: Rotational analogue of law of conservation of linear momentum.

questions.text: Direction of torque is perpendicular to:
questions.explanation: Because it is a cross product (r x F).

questions.text: Dimensions of angular momentum are same as:
questions.explanation: Both are [ML²T⁻¹].

questions.text: The CM of two equal masses lies at:
questions.explanation: For equal masses, (x1+x2)/2 is the center.

questions.text: Moment of inertia is a _____ quantity.
questions.explanation: It depends on the direction of axis, but doesn't follow vector addition laws.

questions.text: The unit of angular momentum is:
questions.explanation: mvr -> kg * m/s * m = kg m²/s.
